commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can make the uncacheable source-fingerprint path non-cacheable and add focused regressions without changing the broader design.; Needs attention: A concrete security-boundary concern remains: uncacheable auth state can still become a stable persisted catalog cache key outside the main ensureOpenClawModelsJson readyCache.; Review finding: [P2] Make uncacheable auth fingerprints non-ca... (sha=2b3d939fd471110d70e5f9437a0d02bce43f874f)
commands.review_summary:
Adds content-validated models-config ready-cache entries, canonical SQLite auth fingerprints, provider-scoped targetProvider/targetModel short-circuiting, caller wiring, proof scripts, and regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +1550, Tests +2032, Other +744. Total +4326 across 23 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: corrupt or unreadable auth state makes buildModelsJsonSourceFingerprint return the same `uncacheable:${agentDir}` value, and two cache consumers can read persisted catalog entries with that key before discovery. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can make the uncacheable source-fingerprint path non-cacheable and add focused regressions without changing the broader design.

target.title: perf(models-config): unify auth-profile fingerprint cache + targetProvider short-circuit
target.body:
## Summary

Improves `models.json` preparation so OpenClaw can skip expensive implicit-provider discovery when the relevant inputs are unchanged, while still re-planning when canonical auth state, plugin catalogs, or generated config inputs drift.

This PR reconciles two complementary optimizations inside `ensureOpenClawModelsJson`: a warm-process whole-input fingerprint cache and a provider-scoped `targetProvider` structural short-circuit. Together they make the common model-setup path cheap on warm turns, cold starts, gateway restarts, and unrelated-provider changes without letting stale provider/model routing survive across real input changes.

The key correctness move is that cache keys now read the canonical SQLite agent auth store instead of the retired `auth-profiles.json` file. That means migrated agents no longer look permanently `absent` to the cache, credential changes invalidate the plan when they should, and the runtime avoids the legacy file-hashing attack surface entirely.

## Why

`ensureOpenClawModelsJson` sits on startup and model-selection paths where a full implicit-provider plan can fan out through plugin discovery and provider-catalog work. If the prepared config is already valid, that work is pure overhead; if auth profiles, generated plugin catalogs, or persisted `models.json` inputs changed, reusing the old plan is a routing-integrity bug.

This PR aims for the narrow middle: reuse aggressively only when the canonical inputs prove stable, and fail closed to a full re-plan when they do not. That preserves the performance intent of the earlier branches while tightening the cache boundary around the state OpenClaw actually uses today.

## What Changed

- Adds an in-process whole-input fingerprint cache for warm `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` calls.
- Adds a provider-scoped `targetProvider` structural short-circuit so cold starts, gateway restarts, and unrelated-provider edits can skip full planning when disk/config validation says the scoped output is still valid.
- Fingerprints the canonical SQLite auth store (`openclaw-agent.sqlite`, via the warm pooled handle) instead of legacy `auth-profiles.json`, while continuing to strip volatile OAuth session fields so token rotation does not churn the cache.
- Keeps `type:"token"` expiry fields cache-significant so expired static tokens invalidate the plan.
- Threads generated plugin catalogs into the pre-plan parsed `models.json` and writes them consistently through skip, noop, and write paths.
- Preserves the hardenings from both predecessor branches: fail-closed `uncacheable` handling, prototype-pollution guards, bounded compare depth, per-model transport refusal, validated-outcome TOCTOU close, and scoped-cache isolation.

## Relationship to Prior PRs

Supersedes #73260 and #73261 with one reconciled implementation. They were complementary, not redundant:

- **#73260** added content-hash auth-profile fingerprinting plus `models.json` drift detection. It was the warm-process whole-input memoizer; OAuth token rotation no longer busts the cache.
- **#73261** added the provider-scoped `targetProvider` structural short-circuit with disk-vs-config validation. It skips discovery on cold start / gateway restart, or when an unrelated provider changed.

Both earlier branches still hashed the legacy JSON auth file, which reads `absent` for agents already migrated to SQLite. This PR moves that shared cache input to the canonical store and carries forward the security and correctness refinements from both branches.

## Verification

- `pnpm tsgo:core` → clean
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test` → clean
- `pnpm vitest run` (the 3 acceptance-criteria files: `models-config.fingerprint-cache.test.ts`, `models-config.write-serialization.test.ts`, `models-config.target-provider-short-circuit.test.ts`) → **112 passed**, including 4 new regression tests pinning sidecar drift + no-DB-create
- `pnpm config:schema:check` → ok
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` → ok
- `pnpm oxlint` (touched files) → clean of new errors (2 pre-existing `no-promise-executor-return` lines are outside this diff)
- `git diff --check` → clean

## Real behavior proof

Real-runtime proof script `scripts/proof-90741-plugin-catalog-cache-validation.ts` drives the real `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` against a real on-disk agent directory, real generated plugin-catalog sidecars, the real `readyCache` populate/drift cycle, and the real no-create auth-store read. **No mocking of the seam under test**; only the network edge is avoided (static `apiKey`, no live provider catalog fetch).

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A plugin-catalog sidecar deleted or tampered after a warm models-config cache entry was created still hit the cache and skipped reconciliation (provider-routing integrity hole, [P1]); separately, the read-only auth fingerprint computation created the agent SQLite DB as a write side effect ([P2]). After the fix, sidecar drift busts the warm cache and the rogue file is reconciled away, and the fingerprint read never materializes the auth DB.
- **Real environment tested:** Real `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` runtime path driven against an on-disk temp agent directory with real generated plugin-catalog sidecars, the real `readyCache` populate/drift cycle, and the real no-create auth-store read. Topic worktree at head `e1524762ec`. No mocking of the seam under test; only the network edge is avoided (static apiKey, no live provider fetch).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node --import tsx scripts/proof-90741-plugin-catalog-cache-validation.ts` (equivalently `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-90741-plugin-catalog-cache-validation.ts`), run from the topic worktree.
- **Evidence after fix:** Captured terminal output (stdout) from the live run of the proof against the real cache code path:

```
[1/3] sidecar-drift-busts-cache-and-reconciles
    rogue sidecar present after re-plan: false
    ok
[2/3] fingerprint-read-does-not-create-agent-db
    auth DB created: false, -wal: false, -shm: false
    ok
[3/3] stable-no-sidecar-still-hits
    models.json mtimeMs cold=1780794326032.3853 warm=1780794326032.3853 ino cold=3879005 warm=3879005
    ok

All runtime assertions passed.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** All three runtime assertions passed (process exit 0). Scenario 1 — the rogue sidecar is absent after re-plan (`rogue sidecar present after re-plan: false`): the warm cache correctly missed on sidecar drift and `removeStalePluginCatalogs` reconciled the tampered file away. Scenario 2 — no `openclaw-agent.sqlite` / `-wal` / `-shm` was created by the fingerprint read (`auth DB created: false, -wal: false, -shm: false`): the read-only cache-key computation no longer has a write side effect. Scenario 3 — steady-state no-sidecar inputs still hit the cache (identical `models.json` inode and mtime cold vs warm), confirming no perf regression.
- **What was not tested:** A fully live multi-provider gateway boot is not exercised; the cache populate/drift/reconcile and no-create auth-read paths are driven end-to-end against real on-disk state by the proof script above. Unit coverage (`models-config.fingerprint-cache.test.ts` + 3 acceptance files, 112 passed) is supplemental.
target.author: zeroaltitude

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
